page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture is supported by 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. It is network and EBS-optimized, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. The combination of high-speed local storage and high network throughput supports data-intensive operations without external storage bottlenecks.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, with a Passmark CPU score in the top 10% at 86426.81 and strong multi-core stress-ng performance. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 331.59 GB/sec for large block read-only operations. In database workloads, the instance achieves 108.32 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and shows strong performance in Passmark database operations.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, massive local NVMe storage, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, the r6idn.32xlarge is designed for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caches like Redis, real-time data analytics, and high-throughput web serving. The hardware profile offers high resource density, balancing compute, memory, and local storage capabilities.
description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized, storage-intensive instance powered by 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores of Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It features 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. With a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, this instance is built for high-throughput networking and storage-heavy operations. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in Passmark CPU tests and strong memory bandwidth, reaching 331.59 GB/sec for large block reads. It also demonstrates strong database capabilities, executing 108.32 million Redis SET operations per second. The r6idn.32xlarge is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, data analytics, and high-throughput web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, 7600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory and storage instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, massive NVMe SSD capacity, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read bandwidth of 331.59 GB/sec
7600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage for high-speed local data access
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high-throughput workloads
Ideal for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching, and data analytics
